zh.lifeSummary: 當Margaret de la Haye於1362年出生時，她的父親Sir Thomas Hay of Locherworth17歲，母親Johanna Gifford16歲。她和William Borthwick of Cactune, 1st of Borthwick至少有2個兒子和2個女兒。她去世於1390年享年28歲，埋葬在英國蘇格蘭。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Margaret de la Haye于1362年出生时，她的父亲Sir Thomas Hay of Locherworth17岁，母亲Johanna Gifford16岁。她和William Borthwick of Cactune, 1st of Borthwick至少有2个儿子和2个女儿。她去世于1390年享年28岁，埋葬在英国苏格兰。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: la
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Hung, Cuong, Anh, Thanh, Ha, Hoang, Tuan, Chau, Hao, Hien, Hoa, Hue, Nam, Phong, Sinh, Tuong, Min, Tam, Tanh, Thai, Tham, Tinh, Yeong.  Chinese Hong, Hang, Sang, Tong, Chan, Chi, Hwa Ja, Hyung, Kam, Ki Bong, Kin, Man.
Vietnamese: from the Chinese surname 羅, see   Luo   1.
Vietnamese (Lã): from the Chinese surname 呂, see Lu 6 and 3 below.

variants.name: Luo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Jian, Yi, Ping, Yong, Zhen, Feng, Hong, Ming, Jin, Bin, Chen, Hai, Lan, Min, Huan, Hung, Shen, Tian.
Chinese:

Mandarin form of the surname 羅, meaning ‘net for catching birds’ or ‘to collect’ in Chinese: (i) from Luo (羅), the name of an ancient state (located in present-day Hubei province). After it was annexed by the state of Chu during the early Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ), people from the state migrated southwards and adopted Luo (羅) as their surname. (ii) from the Po Duo Luo (破多羅) and Chi Luo (叱羅) families from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China, who changed their original surnames to 羅 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) from the personal name Hu Se Luo (斛瑟羅), leader of an ancient Turkic tribe who are believed to have migrated to China during the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ). His descendants inherited his name Hu Se Luo (斛瑟羅) as their surname and shortened it to 羅 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iv) from the name of one of the seven families from the Bandun Man, an ethnic group residing in ancient southwestern China (located mainly in present-day Sichuan province).

nameMeanings.name: Haye
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Hans, Helmut, Willi.

North German, East Frisian, and Dutch: from a personal name, Frisian Hajo, ancient Germanic Haio; or from a short form of the female name Hadewig, composed of the elements hadu ‘battle, strife’ + wīg ‘war’.
German: variant of   Hey   4, 5.

variants.name: Deshayes
meanings: French: topographic name for someone who lived by an enclosure, from the plural of Old French haye ‘hedge’ (see Haye and Hayes ), with fused preposition and definite article des ‘from the’; or a habitational name denoting someone from Les Hayes, the name of several places in various parts of France. Compare Deshaies .

variants.name: Hayenga
meanings: East Frisian: patronymic from the personal name Hajo (see Haye 1). It is formed with the suffix -enga, a variant of -inga (from the patronymic suffix -ing and genitive plural ending -a).

variants.name: Hey
meanings: English (Yorkshire and Lancashire): variant of   Hay  .
Dutch: variant, mostly Americanized (or archaic) and Flemish, of Heij, a topographic name for someone who lived on a heath, Dutch hei, heide.
German: metonymic occupational name for a grower or mower of grass, from Middle High German höu ‘grass, hay’.

variants.name: Hayen
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Alphonse.  German Heinz, Wilhelm.
Flemish, East Frisian, and North German: patronymic from   Haye   1.

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

en.lifeSummary: When Margaret de la Haye was born in 1362, her father, Sir Thomas Hay of Locherworth, was 17 and her mother, Johanna Gifford, was 16. She had at least 2 sons and 2 daughters with William Borthwick of Cactune, 1st of Borthwick. She died in 1390, at the age of 28, and was buried in Scotland, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Margaret de la Haye est née en 1362, son père, Sir Thomas Hay of Locherworth, avait 17 ans et sa mère, Johanna Gifford, avait 16 ans. Elle a eu au moins 2 fils et 2 filles avec William Borthwick of Cactune, 1st of Borthwick. Elle est décédée en 1390, à l’âge de 28 ans, et a été enterrée à Écosse, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als Margaret de la Haye 1362 geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Sir Thomas Hay of Locherworth 17 und ihre Mutter Johanna Gifford 16. Sie hatte mit William Borthwick of Cactune, 1st of Borthwick mindestens 2 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Sie starb 1390 im Alter von 28 Jahren und wurde in Schottland, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Margaret de la Haye è nata il nel 1362, sua padre, Sir Thomas Hay of Locherworth, aveva 17 anni e sua madre, Johanna Gifford, aveva 16 anni. Lei avuto almeno 2 figli e 2 figlie con William Borthwick of Cactune, 1st of Borthwick. Morì nel 1390, all'età di 28 anni, e fu sepolta a Scozia, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Margaret de la Hayeが1362年に生まれた時，父Sir Thomas Hay of Locherworthは17歳，母Johanna Giffordは16歳であった。彼女はWilliam Borthwick of Cactune, 1st of Borthwickとの間に少なくとも2人の息子と2人の娘をもうけた。彼女は1390年に28歳で死去し，イギリススコットランドにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Margaret de la Haye(이)가 1362년에 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir Thomas Hay of Locherworth(은)는 17세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Johanna Gifford(은)는 16세였다. 그녀는 William Borthwick of Cactune, 1st of Borthwick(와)과 적어도 두 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1390년 28세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 스코틀랜드에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Margaret de la Haye nasceu em 1362, seu pai, Sir Thomas Hay of Locherworth, tinha 17 anos e sua mãe, Johanna Gifford, tinha 16 anos. Ela teve pelo menos 2 filhos e 2 filhas com William Borthwick of Cactune, 1st of Borthwick. Ela faleceu em 1390, com 28 anos, e foi sepultada em Escócia, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Margaret de la Haye родилась в 1362 г., её отцу по имени Sir Thomas Hay of Locherworth было 17 лет, а матери, которую звали Johanna Gifford, 16. У неё было по крайней мере 2 сына и 2 дочери от William Borthwick of Cactune, 1st of Borthwick. Она умерла в 1390 г. в возрасте 28 лет и была похоронена в местности Шотландия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Margaret de la Haye nació en 1362, su papá, Sir Thomas Hay of Locherworth, tenía 17 años y su mamá, Johanna Gifford, tenía 16 años. Tuvo por lo menos 2 hijos y 2 hijas con William Borthwick of Cactune, 1st of Borthwick. Murió en 1390, a la edad de 28 años, y fue sepultada en Escocia, Reino Unido.
